display.purpose: Page title only. One instance per view.

title.purpose: Button labels and panel section headings.

body.purpose: Data values, result text, general prose. Max line length 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Field labels and panel section names. Always uppercase, always muted.

mono.purpose: All machine data: JSON payloads, URLs, step counts, pre blocks.

shadows.name: panel-ambient
shadows.purpose: Ambient grounding under glass panels. Not structural elevation.

shadows.name: btn-primary
shadows.purpose: Default shadow under the primary action button.

shadows.name: cursor-glow
shadows.purpose: Glow on the agent cursor overlay dot.

shadows.name: status-dot-running
shadows.purpose: Blue glow on the pulsing running status dot.

shadows.name: status-dot-success
shadows.purpose: Emerald glow on the success status dot.

shadows.name: status-dot-failed
shadows.purpose: Red glow on the failed status dot.

shadows.name: input-focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Soft focus halo on focused input fields.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions and layout changes.

motion.name: ease-lift
motion.purpose: Cursor overlay position transitions. Slight overshoot is intentional — it tracks the agent cursor like a physical pointer.

motion.name: screenshot-fade
motion.purpose: Screenshot transition on new frame. Lingering previous frame avoids flash.

motion.name: pulse-dot
motion.purpose: Running status indicator. Only ambient animation in the system.

motion.name: click-ripple
motion.purpose: Cursor overlay ripple on click action.

motion.name: btn-spin
motion.purpose: Loading spinner inside button while agent is starting.

breakpoints.name: default
breakpoints.note: Dashboard is designed for 1440–1920px monitor widths. No mobile breakpoints; this is an operator tool.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The sole primary CTA. Launches the agent run. Full-width in its container.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #3b82f6 0%, #8b5cf6 100%); color: #f8fafc; border: none; padding: 14px 24px; border-radius: 10px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px; cursor: pointer; width: 100%; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(59,130,246,0.3); transition: all 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 8px 25px rgba(59,130,246,0.4); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; transform: none; background: #334155; box-shadow: none; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3b82f6; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Badge — Running
components.description: Pill badge indicating live agent activity. Pulse dot is the only ambient animation.
components.css: .ds-status-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 6px 14px; border-radius: 99px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-status-running { background: rgba(59,130,246,0.1); color: #60a5fa; border: 1px solid rgba(59,130,246,0.2); } .ds-status-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: #60a5fa; box-shadow: 0 0 10px #60a5fa; animation: ds-pulse 1.5s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0% { transform: scale(0.95); opacity: 0.5; } 50% { transform: scale(1.2); opacity: 1; } 100% { transform: scale(0.95); opacity: 0.5; } }

components.name: Status Badge — Success
components.description: Pill badge for verified successful agent run.
components.css: .ds-status-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 6px 14px; border-radius: 99px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-status-success { background: rgba(16,185,129,0.1); color: #34d399; border: 1px solid rgba(16,185,129,0.2); } .ds-status-dot-success { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; background: #34d399; box-shadow: 0 0 10px #34d399; }

components.name: Glass Panel
components.description: Primary structural container. All data groups live in one. Never nest.
components.html:
SECTION LABEL
Data value here
components.css: .ds-glass-panel { background: rgba(30,41,59,0.7); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(16px); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 20px 40px -10px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); } .ds-data-label { font-size: 12px; color: #94a3b8; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; margin-bottom: 6px; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-data-value { font-size: 14px; color: #f8fafc; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text input for agent task prompt and target URL.
components.css: .ds-input-group { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-field-label { font-size: 12px; color: #94a3b8; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-input { background: rgba(15,23,42,0.6); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color: #f8fafc; padding: 12px 16px; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 14px; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #3b82f6; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(59,130,246,0.15); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #475569; }

components.name: Action Payload Pre
components.description: Monospace preformatted block for JSON action payloads. Most frequently updated surface during a run.
components.css: .ds-action-pre { margin: 0; white-space: pre-wrap; font-size: 13px; color: #a5b4fc; background: rgba(15,23,42,0.8); padding: 16px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace; max-height: 200px; overflow-y: auto; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Result Box — Success
components.description: Inline result summary displayed after a completed agent run. Not a modal.
components.html: Success on attempt 2/5 — extracted 10 rows in 34.2s. Reason: schema valid, row count matches.
components.css: .ds-result { padding: 15px; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.5; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; margin-top: 15px; } .ds-result-success { background: rgba(16,185,129,0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(16,185,129,0.3); color: #a7f3d0; } .ds-result-failed { background: rgba(239,68,68,0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(239,68,68,0.3); color: #fecaca; }

narrative.overview:
AEGIS's dashboard is not a product to be admired — it is a surface to be read. The design language borrows from GitHub's dark-native aesthetic: structured information at high density, functional hierarchy enforced by type weight and color restraint, no decorative noise. The canvas is deep slate, not black. Panels float on it as darker-tinted surfaces with hairline borders. The only saturated colors that appear are status signals — blue for running, emerald for success, red for failure — and they carry meaning every time they fire.

This system explicitly rejects: the rainbow color soup of Grafana-style dashboards, consumer-warm rounded-corner friendliness, glassmorphism used as decoration, hackathon-demo clichés (hero gradients, glowing neon accents, large animated counters for vanity). The agent on screen is doing hard technical work; the interface should look like it agrees.

The visual register is GitHub's internal dark tooling crossed with a terminal-adjacent operator console. Information is dense because the operator needs it dense.
keyCharacteristics: Deep slate canvas, no true black or white
Status signals are the only saturated color moments
Labels uppercase + tracked at 12px; values at 14px regular — always this pairing
Monospace for all machine data: action payloads, JSON, step counts, URLs
Panels at rest are subtle glass surfaces; interactions lift them slightly
Motion is purposeful: cursor overlay, screenshot fade, pulse dot — all carry state

rules.name: The State-Color Purity Rule
rules.body: Blue, violet, emerald, red, and amber appear only as semantic signals — running, action, success, failure, warning. Never decorative. If you reach for one of these colors to make something look nicer, rewrite the element.

rules.name: The Two-Register Rule
rules.body: Every text element is either Prose (Inter) or Machine (JetBrains Mono). There is no third register. A URL in a label uses mono. A button label uses Inter. Never mix them on the same line.

rules.name: The Label Protocol
rules.body: Every data field uses the same two-line pattern: LABEL at 12px uppercase muted, then value at 14px primary below it. This pairing is the atomic unit of the dashboard's information display.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: Shadows appear only on panels (ambient grounding) and on interactive elements at hover. No shadows on text, no inner shadows on inputs, no layered shadow stacks.

dos: Do use the Label Protocol for every data field: 12px uppercase muted label above, 14px primary value below.
Do use JetBrains Mono for all machine data: JSON payloads, URLs, step counts, structured output.
Do keep state colors (blue, violet, emerald, red, amber) as semantic-only signals.
Do use ease-out curves for transitions. State changes should feel resolved, not bouncy.
Do tint every neutral toward the slate hue. No pure #000 or #fff.
Do make failure states legible and prominent. Red badges and result boxes should be as readable as success states.
Do vary spacing for rhythm: 8px micro, 12px control-padding, 20px section breaks, 24px panel gaps, 40px page padding.
donts: Don't use rainbow color palettes or multi-hue overload (Grafana, Datadog). Two neutrals and five semantic colors is the entire palette.
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively. The panel blur is structural; blur on tooltips, dropdowns, or nested cards is decoration. Prohibited.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on any element.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient text. Decorative, never meaningful. Replace with a solid off-white.
Don't nest panels. A glass card inside a glass panel is always wrong.
Don't add modal dialogs as a first solution. Use inline confirmation strips instead.
Don't add animations for decoration. Every animation must communicate state.
Don't use consumer-warm aesthetic choices: bubbly radii, soft pastels, playful illustration.
Don't use the hero-metric template (big number, small label, gradient accent).
